summary: German Renaissance painter, court painter to the Electors of Saxony. Known for portraits of Martin Luther, The Judgment of Paris, The Stag Hunt of Elector Frederick the Wise. Signed works with a winged snake/serpent emblem. Close friend of Luther.

works.name: Portraits of Martin Luther
works.description: Many portraits of Luther in his characteristic floppy black hat. Also painted Luther's wife Katharina von Bora and his parents Hans and Margarethe.

clues.clue: Many portraits of Luther in floppy black hat; also painted wife Katharina von Bora, parents Hans and Margarethe
examples: most famous for creating multiple portraits of his friend Martin Luther
A black hat and a coat are often worn by Katharina von Bora's husband in many of his portraits

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Court painter to Electors of Saxony (Frederick the Wise, etc.). Signed with winged snake/serpent carrying a ruby ring (coat of arms from Frederick III; later changed to single crow's wing after son Hans's death). Close friend of Luther. Shared feast day with Durer (April 6) in Lutheran/Episcopal churches. Sometimes Mayor of Wittenberg. Other works: The Judgment of Paris (Mercury with glass sphere, Paris in red cape and armor), The Stag Hunt of Elector Frederick the Wise, Venus and Cupid (skinny tall nudes), Law and Gospel (consulted Philip Melanchthon), The Fountain of Youth, Nymph of the Spring, Melancholia, Wittenberg Altarpiece, Weimar Altarpiece, Adam and Eve, Venus with Cupid and Honeycomb, The Ill-Matched Couple, Crucifixion (1503).

clues.clue: Signed works with winged snake/serpent carrying ruby ring in its mouth (coat of arms from Frederick the Wise/III); later changed to single crow's wing after son Hans's death
examples: He signed his paintings with a winged snake emblem bestowed on him by Frederick III
After the death of his son Hans, this artist of the Weimar Altarpiece used a single crow's wing in his coat of arms

clues.clue: Court painter to Electors of Saxony; attached to Frederick the Wise's court from 1504
examples: After attracting the attention of Frederick the Wise of Saxony, this man became attached to his court in 1504

clues.clue: The Stag Hunt of Elector Frederick the Wise: archers in bushes, deer chased across river, large party in boat
examples: Archers lie in wait behind bushes in this artist's painting The Stag Hunt of Elector Frederick the Wise

clues.clue: The Judgment of Paris: Mercury holds glass sphere, Paris wears red cape and armor, three naked ladies with necklaces
examples: The god Mercury holds onto a glass sphere and a certain Trojan wears a luxurious red cape and steel plate armor

clues.clue: Skinny, tall nude Eves indistinguishable from his many nude Venuses; Venus and Cupid with Honeycomb
examples: This man's skinny, tall nude Eves are often indistinguishable from his many nude paintings of Venus

clues.clue: Law and Gospel woodcut/painting likely made in consultation with Philip Melanchthon
examples: This artist's painting and woodcut Law and Gospel was likely made in consultation with Philip Melanchthon

clues.clue: The Fountain of Youth: carts carry old women to central rectangular pool
examples: Carts carry old women from the left to the central rectangular pool

clues.clue: Melancholia: woman in red dress whittles stick while nude infants do precocious things (passing ball through hoop)
examples: a woman in a red dress sits on the floor and lazily whittles a stick, seemingly unaware that several nude infants are doing ridiculously precocious things

clues.clue: Wittenberg Altarpiece: Last Supper with self-portrait as waiter; Last Supper shows Jesus dining with Germans
examples: In the Last Supper scene at the center of an altarpiece named for this town, an artist inserted a portrait of himself as a waiter

clues.clue: Shares feast day with Durer on April 6 in Lutheran and Episcopal churches
examples: The Lutheran Church officially venerates Cranach the Elder and this other artist on April 6

clues.clue: Judith with head of Holofernes: wearing big red hat with feathers; 'Power of Women' motif
examples: several paintings of Judith with the head of Holofernes, in which Judith wears a big red hat with feathers

clues.clue: Weimar Altarpiece: blood of Christ splashes onto his own head; completed by his son
examples: The blood of Christ takes an unlikely trajectory and lands on the head of this artist in the Weimar Altarpiece

clues.clue: Adam and Eve against black background; descendants sued for Nazi-stolen version
examples: an Adam and Eve by this friend and portraitist of Martin Luther

clues.clue: Nymph of the Spring: Latin inscription 'I am a nymph of the sacred fountain. Do not disturb my sleep'
examples: A Latin inscription in a painting by this artist translates to 'I am a nymph of the sacred fountain'

comprehensive_summary:
Lucas Cranach the Elder was a German Renaissance painter and court painter to the Electors of Saxony, most commonly identified by his many portraits of Martin Luther and his winged serpent signature.

He signed his works with a winged snake carrying a ruby ring, a coat of arms bestowed by Frederick the Wise (later changed to a single crow's wing after his son Hans's death). He was attached to Frederick the Wise's court from 1504 and was a close friend of Luther, painting him many times in his characteristic floppy black hat, as well as Luther's wife Katharina von Bora.

Key works include The Judgment of Paris (Mercury with glass sphere, Paris in red cape), The Stag Hunt of Elector Frederick the Wise (archers in bushes, deer chased across a river), many skinny tall nudes of Venus (often indistinguishable from his Eves), Venus and Cupid with Honeycomb, Law and Gospel (consulted with Philip Melanchthon), The Fountain of Youth, Melancholia (woman whittles stick while infants do precocious things), and the Wittenberg Altarpiece (self-portrait as waiter in Last Supper; Jesus dines with Germans). He shares a feast day with Durer on April 6.
